It is treated as a Buddhist title of Shinto [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Oyamakui no Kami]]. * {{ill|Sekison Gongen|ja|石尊権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Gongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Seiryū Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:清瀧権現|清瀧権現]]) was enshrined in [[:en:Jingo-ji|Jingo-ji]] in Takao as the tutelary kami of [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] by [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]].<ref>Jaanus, ''Seiryū Gongen'', accessed on October 6, 2008</ref> * {{ill|Shiramine Daigongen|ja|白峯大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiramine Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白峯寺}} ([[:ja:白峯大権現|白峯大権現]]) * {{ill|Suzuka Gongen|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|lt=Suzuka Gongen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:片山神社 (亀山市)|鈴鹿権現]]) – {{ill|Shamanism|ja|巫覡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shamanism|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巫}} ([[:ja:巫覡|巫覡]]) * {{ill|Tateyama Gongen|ja|立山権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:立山権現|立山権現]]) – Tateyama Shugendō ([[:ja:立山修験|立山修験]]) *{{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:瑜伽大権現|瑜伽大権現]]) * {{nihongo|{{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}|蔵王権現}} or {{nihongo|Kongō Zaō Bosatsu|金剛蔵王菩薩}} is a deity worshiped in [[Shugendō]].<ref>Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=201 Zaō Gongen], accessed on October 6, 2008</ref>

([[:ja:羽黒権現|羽黒権現]]) – Haguro Shugendō ([[:ja:羽黒修験|羽黒修験]]) * {{ill|Hakone Gongen|ja|箱根権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakone Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:箱根権現|箱根権現]]) *{{ill|Hakusan Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:ja:飛瀧権現|飛瀧権現]]) * {{ill|Sannō Gongen|ja|山王権現|lt=Hiyoshi Sannō Gongen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannō Gongen|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ([[:ja:山王権現|日吉山王権現]]) – [[:en:Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō|Sannō Shintō]] ([[:ja:山王神道|山王神道]]) * {{ill|Ishizuchi Daigongen|ja|石鎚大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizuchi Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:ja:石鎚大権現|石鎚大権現]]) * {{ill|Isurugi Gongen|ja|石動権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isurugi Gongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Izuna Gongen|ja|飯縄権現|1=Izuna Gongen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuna Gongen|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuna Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:飯縄権現|飯縄権現]]), also called "Izuna Myōjin" and enshrined in Izuna Shrine in [[:en:Nagano, Nagano|Nagano]], is similar to a [[:en:Tengu|Tengu]] and represents the kami of [[:en:Mount Iizuna|Mount Iizuna]].<ref>Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=193 Izuna Gongen], accessed on October 6, 2008</ref> * {{nihongo|{{ill|Izusan Gongen|ja|伊豆山権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izusan Gongen|qq=}}|伊豆山権現}} or {{nihongo|Hashiri-yu Gongen|走湯権現}} is the spirit of a hot spring on Izusan, a hill in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], enshrined in the [[Izusan Shrine|Izusan Jinja]]<ref>Jaanus, ''Izusan Gongen'', accessed on October 6, 2008.</ref> *{{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Gongen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuna Gongen|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuna Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:飯縄権現|飯縄権現]]), also called "Izuna Myōjin" and enshrined in Izuna Shrine in [[:en:Nagano, Nagano|Nagano]], is similar to a [[:en:Tengu|Tengu]] and represents the kami of [[:en:Mount Iizuna|Mount Iizuna]].<ref>Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=193 Izuna Gongen], accessed on October 6, 2008</ref> * {{nihongo|{{ill|Izusan Gongen|ja|伊豆山権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izusan Gongen|qq=}}|伊豆山権現}} or {{nihongo|Hashiri-yu Gongen|走湯権現}} is the spirit of a hot spring on Izusan, a hill in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], enshrined in the [[Izusan Shrine|Izusan Jinja]]<ref>Jaanus, ''Izusan Gongen'', accessed on October 6, 2008.</ref> *{{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|fr|Kasuga_no_Kami|lt=Kasuga Gongen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} *{{ill|Kirishima Rokusha Gongen|ja|霧島六社権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirishima Rokusha Gongen|qq=}} * [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Gongen]] ([[:ja:金毘羅権現|金毘羅権現]]) * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:熊野権現|熊野権現]]), also known as {{nihongo|Three Mountains of Kumano|熊野三山}}.<ref name="kumano">Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=1076 Kumano Shinkō], accessed on October 6, 2008</ref><ref>[http://www.sekaiisan-wakayama.jp/english/s_sanzan.htm Kumano Sanzan] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080609225919/http://www.sekaiisan-wakayama.jp/english/s_sanzan.htm|date=2008-06-09}}, World Heritage Registration Association, accessed on October 13, 2008</ref> The kami enshrined in the three Kumano Sanzan Grand Shrines and worshipped in [[Kumano shrine]]s are the three Kumano mountains: Hongū, Shingū, and Nachi.<ref name="kumano" /> * {{ill|Nezu Gongen|ja|根津神社#歴史|lt=Nezu Gongen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nezu Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:根津神社#歴史|根津権現]]) * {{ill|Ochi Daigongen|ja|越知大権現|1=Ochi Daigongen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi Daigongen|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi Daigongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:越知大権現|越知大権現]]) – Hakusan Shugendō ([[:ja:白山修験|白山修験]]) * {{ill|Omatsu Daigongen|ja|お松大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsu Daigongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Sanki Daigongen|ja|三鬼大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanki Daigongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:三鬼大権現|三鬼大権現]]) * {{nihongo|{{ill|Sannō Gongen|ja|山王権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannō Gongen|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|山王権現}} or {{nihongo|Hie (alternatively ''Hiyoshi'') Sannō Daigongen|日吉山王大権現}} is a guardian deity worshiped in [[:en:Tendai|Tendai]] spread from [[:en:Mount Hiei|Mount Hiei]]. It is treated as a Buddhist title of Shinto [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Oyamakui no Kami]]. * {{ill|Sekison Gongen|ja|石尊権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Gongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Seiryū Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:清瀧権現|清瀧権現]]) was enshrined in [[:en:Jingo-ji|Jingo-ji]] in Takao as the tutelary kami of [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] by [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]].<ref>Jaanus, ''Seiryū Gongen'', accessed on October 6, 2008</ref> * {{ill|Shiramine Daigongen|ja|白峯大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiramine Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白峯寺|1=Shiromine-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579651}} ([[:ja:白峯大権現|白峯大権現]]) * {{ill|Suzuka Gongen|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|lt=Suzuka Gongen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka Gongen|qq=|1=Katayama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594929}} ([[:ja:片山神社 (亀山市)|鈴鹿権現]]) – {{ill|Shamanism|ja|巫覡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shamanism|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巫|1=Wu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3687892}} ([[:ja:巫覡|巫覡]]) * {{ill|Tateyama Gongen|ja|立山権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:立山権現|立山権現]]) – Tateyama Shugendō ([[:ja:立山修験|立山修験]]) *{{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:瑜伽大権現|瑜伽大権現]]) * {{nihongo|{{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}|蔵王権現}} or {{nihongo|Kongō Zaō Bosatsu|金剛蔵王菩薩}} is a deity worshiped in [[Shugendō]].<ref>Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=201 Zaō Gongen], accessed on October 6, 2008</ref>

{{ill|Himuro Gongen|ja|氷室権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷室}} * {{ill|Hirō Gongen|ja|飛瀧権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirō Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:ja:飛瀧権現|飛瀧権現]]) * {{ill|Sannō Gongen|ja|山王権現|lt=Hiyoshi Sannō Gongen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannō Gongen|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ([[:ja:山王権現|日吉山王権現]]) – [[:en:Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō|Sannō Shintō]] ([[:ja:山王神道|山王神道]]) * {{ill|Ishizuchi Daigongen|ja|石鎚大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizuchi Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:ja:石鎚大権現|石鎚大権現]]) * {{ill|Isurugi Gongen|ja|石動権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isurugi Gongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Izuna Gongen|ja|飯縄権現|1=Izuna Gongen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuna Gongen|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuna Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:飯縄権現|飯縄権現]]), also called "Izuna Myōjin" and enshrined in Izuna Shrine in [[:en:Nagano, Nagano|Nagano]], is similar to a [[:en:Tengu|Tengu]] and represents the kami of [[:en:Mount Iizuna|Mount Iizuna]].<ref>Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=193 Izuna Gongen], accessed on October 6, 2008</ref> * {{nihongo|{{ill|Izusan Gongen|ja|伊豆山権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izusan Gongen|qq=}}|伊豆山権現}} or {{nihongo|Hashiri-yu Gongen|走湯権現}} is the spirit of a hot spring on Izusan, a hill in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], enshrined in the [[Izusan Shrine|Izusan Jinja]]<ref>Jaanus, ''Izusan Gongen'', accessed on October 6, 2008.</ref> *{{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|fr|Kasuga_no_Kami|lt=Kasuga Gongen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} *{{ill|Kirishima Rokusha Gongen|ja|霧島六社権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirishima Rokusha Gongen|qq=}} * [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Gongen]] ([[:ja:金毘羅権現|金毘羅権現]]) * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:熊野権現|熊野権現]]), also known as {{nihongo|Three Mountains of Kumano|熊野三山}}.<ref name="kumano">Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=1076 Kumano Shinkō], accessed on October 6, 2008</ref><ref>[http://www.sekaiisan-wakayama.jp/english/s_sanzan.htm Kumano Sanzan] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080609225919/http://www.sekaiisan-wakayama.jp/english/s_sanzan.htm|date=2008-06-09}}, World Heritage Registration Association, accessed on October 13, 2008</ref> The kami enshrined in the three Kumano Sanzan Grand Shrines and worshipped in [[Kumano shrine]]s are the three Kumano mountains: Hongū, Shingū, and Nachi.<ref name="kumano" /> * {{ill|Nezu Gongen|ja|根津神社#歴史|lt=Nezu Gongen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nezu Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:根津神社#歴史|根津権現]]) * {{ill|Ochi Daigongen|ja|越知大権現|1=Ochi Daigongen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi Daigongen|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi Daigongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:越知大権現|越知大権現]]) – Hakusan Shugendō ([[:ja:白山修験|白山修験]]) * {{ill|Omatsu Daigongen|ja|お松大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsu Daigongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Sanki Daigongen|ja|三鬼大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanki Daigongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:三鬼大権現|三鬼大権現]]) * {{nihongo|{{ill|Sannō Gongen|ja|山王権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannō Gongen|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|山王権現}} or {{nihongo|Hie (alternatively ''Hiyoshi'') Sannō Daigongen|日吉山王大権現}} is a guardian deity worshiped in [[:en:Tendai|Tendai]] spread from [[:en:Mount Hiei|Mount Hiei]]. It is treated as a Buddhist title of Shinto [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Oyamakui no Kami]]. * {{ill|Sekison Gongen|ja|石尊権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Gongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Seiryū Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:清瀧権現|清瀧権現]]) was enshrined in [[:en:Jingo-ji|Jingo-ji]] in Takao as the tutelary kami of [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] by [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]].<ref>Jaanus, ''Seiryū Gongen'', accessed on October 6, 2008</ref> * {{ill|Shiramine Daigongen|ja|白峯大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiramine Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白峯寺|1=Shiromine-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579651}} ([[:ja:白峯大権現|白峯大権現]]) * {{ill|Suzuka Gongen|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|lt=Suzuka Gongen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka Gongen|qq=|1=Katayama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594929}} ([[:ja:片山神社 (亀山市)|鈴鹿権現]]) – {{ill|Shamanism|ja|巫覡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shamanism|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巫|1=Wu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3687892}} ([[:ja:巫覡|巫覡]]) * {{ill|Tateyama Gongen|ja|立山権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:立山権現|立山権現]]) – Tateyama Shugendō ([[:ja:立山修験|立山修験]]) *{{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:瑜伽大権現|瑜伽大権現]]) * {{nihongo|{{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}|蔵王権現}} or {{nihongo|Kongō Zaō Bosatsu|金剛蔵王菩薩}} is a deity worshiped in [[Shugendō]].<ref>Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=201 Zaō Gongen], accessed on October 6, 2008</ref>

[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=193 Izuna Gongen], accessed on October 6, 2008</ref> * {{nihongo|{{ill|Izusan Gongen|ja|伊豆山権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izusan Gongen|qq=}}|伊豆山権現}} or {{nihongo|Hashiri-yu Gongen|走湯権現}} is the spirit of a hot spring on Izusan, a hill in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], enshrined in the [[Izusan Shrine|Izusan Jinja]]<ref>Jaanus, ''Izusan Gongen'', accessed on October 6, 2008.</ref> *{{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Gongen|ja|熊野権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:熊野権現|熊野権現]]), also known as {{nihongo|Three Mountains of Kumano|熊野三山}}.<ref name="kumano">Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=1076 Kumano Shinkō], accessed on October 6, 2008</ref><ref>[http://www.sekaiisan-wakayama.jp/english/s_sanzan.htm Kumano Sanzan] 2008</ref> The kami enshrined in the three Kumano Sanzan Grand Shrines and worshipped in [[Kumano shrine]]s are the three Kumano mountains: Hongū, Shingū, and Nachi.<ref name="kumano" /> * {{ill|Nezu Gongen|ja|根津神社#歴史|lt=Nezu Gongen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nezu Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:根津神社#歴史|根津権現]]) * {{ill|Ochi Daigongen|ja|越知大権現|1=Ochi Daigongen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi Daigongen|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi Daigongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:越知大権現|越知大権現]]) – Hakusan Shugendō ([[:ja:白山修験|白山修験]]) * {{ill|Omatsu Daigongen|ja|お松大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsu Daigongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Sanki Daigongen|ja|三鬼大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanki Daigongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:三鬼大権現|三鬼大権現]]) * is a guardian deity worshiped in [[:en:Tendai|Tendai]] spread from [[:en:Mount Hiei|Mount Hiei]]. It is treated as a Buddhist title of Shinto [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Oyamakui no Kami]]. * {{ill|Sekison Gongen|ja|石尊権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Gongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Seiryū Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:清瀧権現|清瀧権現]]) was enshrined in [[:en:Jingo-ji|Jingo-ji]] in Takao as the tutelary kami of [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] by [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]].<ref>Jaanus, ''Seiryū Gongen'', accessed on October 6, 2008</ref> * {{ill|Shiramine Daigongen|ja|白峯大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiramine Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白峯寺|1=Shiromine-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579651}} ([[:ja:白峯大権現|白峯大権現]]) * {{ill|Suzuka Gongen|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|lt=Suzuka Gongen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka Gongen|qq=|1=Katayama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594929}} ([[:ja:片山神社 (亀山市)|鈴鹿権現]]) – {{ill|Shamanism|ja|巫覡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shamanism|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巫|1=Wu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3687892}} ([[:ja:巫覡|巫覡]]) * {{ill|Tateyama Gongen|ja|立山権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:立山権現|立山権現]]) – Tateyama Shugendō ([[:ja:立山修験|立山修験]]) *{{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:瑜伽大権現|瑜伽大権現]]) * {{nihongo|{{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}|蔵王権現}} or {{nihongo|Kongō Zaō Bosatsu|金剛蔵王菩薩}} is a deity worshiped in [[Shugendō]].<ref>Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=201 Zaō Gongen], accessed on October 6, 2008</ref>

{{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080609225919/http://www.sekaiisan-wakayama.jp/english/s_sanzan.htm|date=2008-06-09}}, World Heritage Registration Association, accessed on October 13, 2008</ref> The kami enshrined in the three Kumano Sanzan Grand Shrines and worshipped in [[Kumano shrine]]s are the three Kumano mountains: Hongū, Shingū, and Nachi.<ref name="kumano" /> * {{ill|Nezu Gongen|ja|根津神社#歴史|lt=Nezu Gongen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nezu Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:根津神社#歴史|根津権現]]) * {{ill|Ochi Daigongen|ja|越知大権現|1=Ochi Daigongen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi Daigongen|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi Daigongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:越知大権現|越知大権現]]) – Hakusan Shugendō ([[:ja:白山修験|白山修験]]) * {{ill|Omatsu Daigongen|ja|お松大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsu Daigongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Sanki Daigongen|ja|三鬼大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanki Daigongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:三鬼大権現|三鬼大権現]]) * {{nihongo|{{ill|Sannō Gongen|ja|山王権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannō Gongen|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|山王権現}} or {{nihongo|Hie (alternatively ''Hiyoshi'') Sannō Daigongen|日吉山王大権現}} is a guardian deity worshiped in [[:en:Tendai|Tendai]] spread from [[:en:Mount Hiei|Mount Hiei]]. It is treated as a Buddhist title of Shinto [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Oyamakui no Kami]]. * {{ill|Sekison Gongen|ja|石尊権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Gongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Seiryū Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:清瀧権現|清瀧権現]]) was enshrined in [[:en:Jingo-ji|Jingo-ji]] in Takao as the tutelary kami of [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] by [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]].<ref>Jaanus, ''Seiryū Gongen'', accessed on October 6, 2008</ref> * {{ill|Shiramine Daigongen|ja|白峯大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiramine Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白峯寺|1=Shiromine-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579651}} ([[:ja:白峯大権現|白峯大権現]]) * {{ill|Suzuka Gongen|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|lt=Suzuka Gongen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka Gongen|qq=|1=Katayama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594929}} ([[:ja:片山神社 (亀山市)|鈴鹿権現]]) – {{ill|Shamanism|ja|巫覡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shamanism|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巫|1=Wu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3687892}} ([[:ja:巫覡|巫覡]]) * {{ill|Tateyama Gongen|ja|立山権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:立山権現|立山権現]]) – Tateyama Shugendō ([[:ja:立山修験|立山修験]]) *{{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:瑜伽大権現|瑜伽大権現]]) * {{nihongo|{{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}|蔵王権現}} or {{nihongo|Kongō Zaō Bosatsu|金剛蔵王菩薩}} is a deity worshiped in [[Shugendō]].<ref>Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=201 Zaō Gongen], accessed on October 6, 2008</ref>

It is treated as a Buddhist title of Shinto [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Oyamakui no Kami]]. * {{ill|Sekison Gongen|ja|石尊権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Gongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Seiryū Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:清瀧権現|清瀧権現]]) was enshrined in [[:en:Jingo-ji|Jingo-ji]] in Takao as the tutelary kami of [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] by [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]].<ref>Jaanus, ''Seiryū Gongen'', accessed on October 6, 2008</ref> * {{ill|Shiramine Daigongen|ja|白峯大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiramine Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白峯寺|1=Shiromine-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579651}} ([[:ja:白峯大権現|白峯大権現]]) * {{ill|Suzuka Gongen|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|lt=Suzuka Gongen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka Gongen|qq=|1=Katayama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594929}} ([[:ja:片山神社 (亀山市)|鈴鹿権現]]) – {{ill|Shamanism|ja|巫覡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shamanism|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巫|1=Wu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3687892}} ([[:ja:巫覡|巫覡]]) * {{ill|Tateyama Gongen|ja|立山権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:立山権現|立山権現]]) – Tateyama Shugendō ([[:ja:立山修験|立山修験]]) *{{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:瑜伽大権現|瑜伽大権現]]) * {{nihongo|{{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}|蔵王権現}} or {{nihongo|Kongō Zaō Bosatsu|金剛蔵王菩薩}} is a deity worshiped in [[Shugendō]].<ref>Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=201 Zaō Gongen], accessed on October 6, 2008</ref>

It is treated as a Buddhist title of Shinto [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Oyamakui no Kami]]. * {{ill|Sekison Gongen|ja|石尊権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Gongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Seiryū Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:清瀧権現|清瀧権現]]) was enshrined in [[:en:Jingo-ji|Jingo-ji]] in Takao as the tutelary kami of [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] by [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]].<ref>Jaanus, ''Seiryū Gongen'', accessed on October 6, 2008</ref> * {{ill|Shiramine Daigongen|ja|白峯大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiramine Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白峯寺|1=Shiromine-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579651}} ([[:ja:白峯大権現|白峯大権現]]) * {{ill|Suzuka Gongen|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|lt=Suzuka Gongen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka Gongen|qq=|1=Katayama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594929}} ([[:ja:片山神社 (亀山市)|鈴鹿権現]]) – {{ill|Shamanism|ja|巫覡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shamanism|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巫|1=Wu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3687892}} ([[:ja:巫覡|巫覡]]) * {{ill|Tateyama Gongen|ja|立山権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:立山権現|立山権現]]) – Tateyama Shugendō ([[:ja:立山修験|立山修験]]) *{{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:瑜伽大権現|瑜伽大権現]]) * {{nihongo|{{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}|蔵王権現}} or {{nihongo|Kongō Zaō Bosatsu|金剛蔵王菩薩}} is a deity worshiped in [[Shugendō]].<ref>Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=201 Zaō Gongen], accessed on October 6, 2008</ref>

Gongen|ja|石動権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isurugi Gongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Izuna Gongen|ja|飯縄権現|1=Izuna Gongen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuna Gongen|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuna Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:飯縄権現|飯縄権現]]), also called "Izuna Myōjin" and enshrined in Izuna Shrine in [[:en:Nagano, Nagano|Nagano]], is similar to a [[:en:Tengu|Tengu]] and represents the kami of [[:en:Mount Iizuna|Mount Iizuna]].<ref>Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=193 Izuna Gongen], accessed on October 6, 2008</ref> * {{nihongo|{{ill|Izusan Gongen|ja|伊豆山権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izusan Gongen|qq=}}|伊豆山権現}} or {{nihongo|Hashiri-yu Gongen|走湯権現}} is the spirit of a hot spring on Izusan, a hill in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], enshrined in the [[Izusan Shrine|Izusan Jinja]]<ref>Jaanus, ''Izusan Gongen'', accessed on October 6, 2008.</ref> *{{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|fr|Kasuga_no_Kami|lt=Kasuga Gongen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} *{{ill|Kirishima Rokusha Gongen|ja|霧島六社権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirishima Rokusha Gongen|qq=}} * [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Gongen]] ([[:ja:金毘羅権現|金毘羅権現]]) * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:熊野権現|熊野権現]]), also known as {{nihongo|Three Mountains of Kumano|熊野三山}}.<ref name="kumano">Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=1076 Kumano Shinkō], accessed on October 6, 2008</ref><ref>[http://www.sekaiisan-wakayama.jp/english/s_sanzan.htm Kumano Sanzan] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080609225919/http://www.sekaiisan-wakayama.jp/english/s_sanzan.htm|date=2008-06-09}}, World Heritage Registration Association, accessed on October 13, 2008</ref> The kami enshrined in the three Kumano Sanzan Grand Shrines and worshipped in [[Kumano shrine]]s are the three Kumano mountains: Hongū, Shingū, and Nachi.<ref name="kumano" /> * {{ill|Nezu Gongen|ja|根津神社#歴史|lt=Nezu Gongen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nezu Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:根津神社#歴史|根津権現]]) * {{ill|Ochi Daigongen|ja|越知大権現|1=Ochi Daigongen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi Daigongen|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi Daigongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:越知大権現|越知大権現]]) – Hakusan Shugendō ([[:ja:白山修験|白山修験]]) * {{ill|Omatsu Daigongen|ja|お松大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsu Daigongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Sanki Daigongen|ja|三鬼大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanki Daigongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:三鬼大権現|三鬼大権現]]) * {{nihongo|{{ill|Sannō Gongen|ja|山王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannō Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|山王権現}} or {{nihongo|Hie (alternatively ''Hiyoshi'') Sannō Daigongen|日吉山王大権現}} is a guardian deity worshiped in [[:en:Tendai|Tendai]] spread from [[:en:Mount Hiei|Mount Hiei]]. It is treated as a Buddhist title of Shinto [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Oyamakui no Kami]]. * {{ill|Sekison Gongen|ja|石尊権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Gongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Seiryū Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:清瀧権現|清瀧権現]]) was enshrined in [[:en:Jingo-ji|Jingo-ji]] in Takao as the tutelary kami of [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] by [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]].<ref>Jaanus, ''Seiryū Gongen'', accessed on October 6, 2008</ref> * {{ill|Shiramine Daigongen|ja|白峯大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiramine Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白峯寺|1=Shiromine-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579651}} ([[:ja:白峯大権現|白峯大権現]]) * {{ill|Suzuka Gongen|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|lt=Suzuka Gongen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka Gongen|1=Katayama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594929}} ([[:ja:片山神社 (亀山市)|鈴鹿権現]]) – {{ill|Shamanism|ja|巫覡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shamanism|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巫|1=Wu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3687892}} ([[:ja:巫覡|巫覡]]) * {{ill|Tateyama Gongen|ja|立山権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:立山権現|立山権現]]) – Tateyama Shugendō ([[:ja:立山修験|立山修験]]) *{{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:瑜伽大権現|瑜伽大権現]]) * {{nihongo|{{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}|蔵王権現}} or {{nihongo|Kongō Zaō Bosatsu|金剛蔵王菩薩}} is a deity worshiped in [[Shugendō]].<ref>Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=201 Zaō Gongen], accessed on October 6, 2008</ref>

Gongen|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuna Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:飯縄権現|飯縄権現]]), also called "Izuna Myōjin" and enshrined in Izuna Shrine in [[:en:Nagano, Nagano|Nagano]], is similar to a {{ill|Tengu|en|Tengu|WD=Q742360|lt=Tengu}} and represents the kami of {{ill|Mount Iizuna|en|Mount Iizuna|WD=Q11666652|lt=Mount Iizuna}}.<ref>Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=193 Izuna Gongen], accessed on October 6, 2008</ref> * {{nihongo|{{ill|Izusan Gongen|ja|伊豆山権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izusan Gongen|qq=}}|伊豆山権現}} or {{nihongo|Hashiri-yu Gongen|走湯権現}} is the spirit of a hot spring on Izusan, a hill in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, enshrined in the [[Izusan Shrine|Izusan Jinja]]<ref>Jaanus, ''Izusan Gongen'', accessed on October 6, 2008.</ref> *{{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=1076 Kumano Shinkō], accessed on October 6, 2008</ref><ref>[http://www.sekaiisan-wakayama.jp/english/s_sanzan.htm Kumano Sanzan] 2008</ref> The kami enshrined in the three Kumano Sanzan Grand Shrines and worshipped in [[Kumano shrine]]s are the three Kumano mountains: Hongū, Shingū, and Nachi.<ref name="kumano" /> * {{ill|Nezu Gongen|ja|根津神社#歴史|lt=Nezu Gongen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nezu Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:根津神社#歴史|根津権現]]) * {{ill|Ochi Daigongen|ja|越知大権現|1=Ochi Daigongen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi is a redirect}}|山王権現}} or {{nihongo|Hie (alternatively ''Hiyoshi'') Sannō Daigongen|日吉山王大権現}} is a guardian deity worshiped in {{ill|Tendai|en|Tendai|WD=Q855964|lt=Tendai}} spread from {{ill|Mount Hiei|en|Mount Hiei|WD=Q748040|lt=Mount Hiei}}. It is treated as a Buddhist title of Shinto {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|en|Oyamakui no Kami|WD=Q10935105|lt=Oyamakui no Kami}}. * {{ill|Sekison Gongen|ja|石尊権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Gongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Seiryū Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:清瀧権現|清瀧権現]]) was enshrined in {{ill|Jingo-ji|en|Jingo-ji|WD=Q1075034|lt=Jingo-ji}} in Takao as the tutelary kami of {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism}} by {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai}}.<ref>Jaanus, ''Seiryū Gongen'', accessed on October 6, 2008</ref> * {{ill|q|lt=Shiramine Daigongen|ja|白峯大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiramine Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白峯寺|1=Shiromine-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579651}} ([[:ja:白峯大権現|白峯大権現]]) * {{ill|Suzuka Gongen|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|lt=Suzuka Gongen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka Gongen|1=Katayama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594929}} ([[:ja:片山神社 (亀山市)|鈴鹿権現]]) – {{ill|Shamanism|ja|巫覡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shamanism|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巫|1=Wu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3687892}} ([[:ja:巫覡|巫覡]]) * {{ill|Tateyama Gongen|ja|立山権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:立山権現|立山権現]]) – Tateyama Shugendō ([[:ja:立山修験|立山修験]]) *{{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:瑜伽大権現|瑜伽大権現]]) * {{nihongo|{{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}|蔵王権現}} or {{nihongo|Kongō Zaō Bosatsu|金剛蔵王菩薩}} is a deity worshiped in [[Shugendō]].<ref>Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=201 Zaō Gongen], accessed on October 6, 2008</ref>

Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:熊野権現|熊野権現]]), also known as {{nihongo|Three Mountains of Kumano|熊野三山}}.<ref name="kumano">Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=1076 Kumano Shinkō], accessed on October 6, 2008</ref><ref>[http://www.sekaiisan-wakayama.jp/english/s_sanzan.htm Kumano Sanzan] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080609225919/http://www.sekaiisan-wakayama.jp/english/s_sanzan.htm|date=2008-06-09}}, World Heritage Registration Association, accessed on October 13, 2008</ref> The kami enshrined in the three Kumano Sanzan Grand Shrines and worshipped in [[Kumano shrine]]s are the three Kumano mountains: Hongū, Shingū, and Nachi.<ref name="kumano" /> * {{ill|Nezu Gongen|ja|根津神社#歴史|lt=Nezu Gongen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nezu Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:根津神社#歴史|根津権現]]) * {{ill|Ochi Daigongen|ja|越知大権現|1=Ochi Daigongen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi Daigongen|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi Daigongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:越知大権現|越知大権現]]) – Hakusan Shugendō ([[:ja:白山修験|白山修験]]) * {{ill|Omatsu Daigongen|ja|お松大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsu Daigongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Sanki Daigongen|ja|三鬼大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanki Daigongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:三鬼大権現|三鬼大権現]]) * {{nihongo|{{ill|Sannō Gongen|ja|山王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannō Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|山王権現}} or {{nihongo|Hie (alternatively ''Hiyoshi'') Sannō Daigongen|日吉山王大権現}} is a guardian deity worshiped in {{ill|Tendai|en|Tendai|WD=Q855964|lt=Tendai}} spread from {{ill|Mount Hiei|en|Mount Hiei|WD=Q748040|lt=Mount Hiei}}. It is treated as a Buddhist title of Shinto {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|en|Oyamakui no Kami|WD=Q10935105|lt=Oyamakui no Kami}}. * {{ill|Sekison Gongen|ja|石尊権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Gongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Seiryū Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:清瀧権現|清瀧権現]]) was enshrined in {{ill|Jingo-ji|en|Jingo-ji|WD=Q1075034|lt=Jingo-ji}} in Takao as the tutelary kami of {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism}} by {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai}}.<ref>Jaanus, ''Seiryū Gongen'', accessed on October 6, 2008</ref> * {{ill|q|lt=Shiramine Daigongen|ja|白峯大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiramine Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白峯寺|1=Shiromine-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579651}} ([[:ja:白峯大権現|白峯大権現]]) * {{ill|Suzuka Gongen|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|lt=Suzuka Gongen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka Gongen|1=Katayama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594929}} ([[:ja:片山神社 (亀山市)|鈴鹿権現]]) – {{ill|Shamanism|ja|巫覡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shamanism|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巫|1=Wu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3687892}} ([[:ja:巫覡|巫覡]]) * {{ill|Tateyama Gongen|ja|立山権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:立山権現|立山権現]]) – Tateyama Shugendō ([[:ja:立山修験|立山修験]]) *{{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:瑜伽大権現|瑜伽大権現]]) * {{nihongo|{{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}|蔵王権現}} or {{nihongo|Kongō Zaō Bosatsu|金剛蔵王菩薩}} is a deity worshiped in [[Shugendō]].<ref>Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=201 Zaō Gongen], accessed on October 6, 2008</ref>

Daigongen|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi Daigongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:越知大権現|越知大権現]]) – Hakusan Shugendō ([[:ja:白山修験|白山修験]]) * {{ill|Omatsu Daigongen|ja|お松大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsu Daigongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Sanki Daigongen|ja|三鬼大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanki Daigongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:三鬼大権現|三鬼大権現]]) * {{nihongo|{{ill|Sannō Gongen|ja|山王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannō Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|山王権現}} or {{nihongo|Hie (alternatively ''Hiyoshi'') Sannō Daigongen|日吉山王大権現}} is a guardian deity worshiped in {{ill|Tendai|en|Tendai|WD=Q855964|lt=Tendai}} spread from {{ill|Mount Hiei|en|Mount Hiei|WD=Q748040|lt=Mount Hiei}}. It is treated as a Buddhist title of Shinto {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|en|Oyamakui no Kami|WD=Q10935105|lt=Oyamakui no Kami}}. * {{ill|Sekison Gongen|ja|石尊権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Gongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Seiryū Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:清瀧権現|清瀧権現]]) was enshrined in {{ill|Jingo-ji|en|Jingo-ji|WD=Q1075034|lt=Jingo-ji}} in Takao as the tutelary kami of {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism}} by {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai}}.<ref>Jaanus, ''Seiryū Gongen'', accessed on October 6, 2008</ref> * {{ill|q|lt=Shiramine Daigongen|ja|白峯大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiramine Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白峯寺|1=Shiromine-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579651}} ([[:ja:白峯大権現|白峯大権現]]) * {{ill|Suzuka Gongen|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|lt=Suzuka Gongen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka Gongen|1=Katayama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594929}} ([[:ja:片山神社 (亀山市)|鈴鹿権現]]) – {{ill|Shamanism|ja|巫覡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shamanism|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巫|1=Wu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3687892}} ([[:ja:巫覡|巫覡]]) * {{ill|Tateyama Gongen|ja|立山権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:立山権現|立山権現]]) – Tateyama Shugendō ([[:ja:立山修験|立山修験]]) *{{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:瑜伽大権現|瑜伽大権現]]) * {{nihongo|{{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}|蔵王権現}} or {{nihongo|Kongō Zaō Bosatsu|金剛蔵王菩薩}} is a deity worshiped in [[Shugendō]].<ref>Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=201 Zaō Gongen], accessed on October 6, 2008</ref>

([[:ja:愛宕権現|愛宕権現]]) *{{ill|Chimyō Gongen|ja|智明権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chimyō Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:智明権現|智明権現]]) *{{ill|Chōkai Shugendō|ja|鳥海修験|lt=Chōkai-san Daigongen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkai Shugendō|qq=}} *{{ill|Futarasan Gongen|ja|二荒権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futarasan Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:ja:二荒権現|二荒権現]]) – Nikkō Shugendō ([[:ja:日光修験|日光修験]]) *{{ill|Hachiōji Gongen|ja|八王子権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:八王子権現|八王子権現]]) *{{ill|Haguro Gongen|ja|羽黒権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:羽黒権現|羽黒権現]]) – Haguro Shugendō ([[:ja:羽黒修験|羽黒修験]]) * {{ill|Hakone Gongen|ja|箱根権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakone Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:箱根権現|箱根権現]]) *{{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:白山権現|白山権現]]) – Hakusan Shugendō ([[:ja:白山修験|白山修験]]) * {{ill|Mount Hiko|en|Mount Hiko|WD=Q3077886|lt=Hikosan Gongen|1=Mount Hiko}} ([[:ja:英彦山|英彦山権現]]) * {{ill|Himuro Gongen|ja|氷室権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷室}} * {{ill|Hirō Gongen|ja|飛瀧権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirō Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:ja:飛瀧権現|飛瀧権現]]) * {{ill|Sannō Gongen|ja|山王権現|lt=Hiyoshi Sannō Gongen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannō Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ([[:ja:山王権現|日吉山王権現]]) – {{ill|Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō|en|Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō|WD=Q4407806|lt=Sannō Shintō|1=Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō}} ([[:ja:山王神道|山王神道]]) * {{ill|Ishizuchi Daigongen|ja|石鎚大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizuchi Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:ja:石鎚大権現|石鎚大権現]]) * {{ill|Isurugi Gongen|ja|石動権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isurugi Gongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Izuna Gongen|ja|飯縄権現|1=Izuna Gongen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuna Gongen|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuna Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:飯縄権現|飯縄権現]]), also called "Izuna Myōjin" and enshrined in Izuna Shrine in [[:en:Nagano, Nagano|Nagano]], is similar to a {{ill|Tengu|en|Tengu|WD=Q742360|lt=Tengu|1=Tengu}} and represents the kami of {{ill|Mount Iizuna|en|Mount Iizuna|WD=Q11666652|lt=Mount Iizuna|1=Mount Iizuna}}.<ref>Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=193 Izuna Gongen], accessed on October 6, 2008</ref> * {{nihongo|{{ill|Izusan Gongen|ja|伊豆山権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izusan Gongen|qq=}}|伊豆山権現}} or {{nihongo|Hashiri-yu Gongen|走湯権現}} is the spirit of a hot spring on Izusan, a hill in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, enshrined in the [[Izusan Shrine|Izusan Jinja]]<ref>Jaanus, ''Izusan Gongen'', accessed on October 6, 2008.</ref> *{{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|fr|Kasuga_no_Kami|lt=Kasuga Gongen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} *{{ill|Kirishima Rokusha Gongen|ja|霧島六社権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirishima Rokusha Gongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Konpira Gongen|en|Konpira Gongen|WD=Q167077|lt=Konpira Gongen|1=Konpira Gongen}} ([[:ja:金毘羅権現|金毘羅権現]]) * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:熊野権現|熊野権現]]), also known as {{nihongo|Three Mountains of Kumano|熊野三山}}.<ref name="kumano">Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=1076 Kumano Shinkō], accessed on October 6, 2008</ref><ref>[http://www.sekaiisan-wakayama.jp/english/s_sanzan.htm Kumano Sanzan] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080609225919/http://www.sekaiisan-wakayama.jp/english/s_sanzan.htm|date=2008-06-09}}, World Heritage Registration Association, accessed on October 13, 2008</ref> The kami enshrined in the three Kumano Sanzan Grand Shrines and worshipped in [[Kumano shrine]]s are the three Kumano mountains: Hongū, Shingū, and Nachi.<ref name="kumano" /> * {{ill|Nezu Gongen|ja|根津神社#歴史|lt=Nezu Gongen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nezu Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:根津神社#歴史|根津権現]]) * {{ill|Ochi Daigongen|ja|越知大権現|1=Ochi Daigongen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi Daigongen|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi Daigongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:越知大権現|越知大権現]]) – Hakusan Shugendō ([[:ja:白山修験|白山修験]]) * {{ill|Omatsu Daigongen|ja|お松大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omatsu Daigongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Sanki Daigongen|ja|三鬼大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanki Daigongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:三鬼大権現|三鬼大権現]]) * {{nihongo|{{ill|Sannō Gongen|ja|山王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannō Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|山王権現}} or {{nihongo|Hie (alternatively ''Hiyoshi'') Sannō Daigongen|日吉山王大権現}} is a guardian deity worshiped in {{ill|Tendai|en|Tendai|WD=Q855964|lt=Tendai|1=Tendai}} spread from {{ill|Mount Hiei|en|Mount Hiei|WD=Q748040|lt=Mount Hiei|1=Mount Hiei}}. It is treated as a Buddhist title of Shinto {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|en|Oyamakui no Kami|WD=Q10935105|lt=Oyamakui no Kami|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}. * {{ill|Sekison Gongen|ja|石尊権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekison Gongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Seiryū Gongen|ja|清瀧権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiryū Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:清瀧権現|清瀧権現]]) was enshrined in {{ill|Jingo-ji|en|Jingo-ji|WD=Q1075034|lt=Jingo-ji|1=Jingo-ji}} in Takao as the tutelary kami of {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism|1=Shingon Buddhism}} by {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}}.<ref>Jaanus, ''Seiryū Gongen'', accessed on October 6, 2008</ref> * {{ill|q|lt=Shiramine Daigongen|ja|白峯大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiramine Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白峯寺|1=Shiromine-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579651}} ([[:ja:白峯大権現|白峯大権現]]) * {{ill|Suzuka Gongen|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|lt=Suzuka Gongen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuka Gongen|1=Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594929}} ([[:ja:片山神社 (亀山市)|鈴鹿権現]]) – {{ill|Shamanism|ja|巫覡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shamanism|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巫|1=Wu (shaman)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3687892}} ([[:ja:巫覡|巫覡]]) * {{ill|Tateyama Gongen|ja|立山権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Gongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:立山権現|立山権現]]) – Tateyama Shugendō ([[:ja:立山修験|立山修験]]) *{{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} ([[:ja:瑜伽大権現|瑜伽大権現]]) * {{nihongo|{{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}}|蔵王権現}} or {{nihongo|Kongō Zaō Bosatsu|金剛蔵王菩薩}} is a deity worshiped in [[Shugendō]].<ref>Encyclopedia of Shinto, [http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=201 Zaō Gongen], accessed on October 6, 2008</ref>
